title: From Precision Illusion to Calibration Intervention: A Framework for Evaluating and Correcting LLM Uncertainty Expression in Safety-Critical Advice

citation.apa: Guo, M. (2026). From Precision Illusion to Calibration Intervention.
citation.chicago: Guo, Max. "From Precision Illusion to Calibration Intervention." 2026.
citation.mla: Guo, Max. "From Precision Illusion to Calibration Intervention." 2026.

abstract: Large language models are increasingly used as advisors in safety-critical domains, but standard benchmarks measure accuracy rather than calibration. We introduce the UCS taxonomy, evaluate 126 fitness advice questions across two models (finding the precision illusion: 92.9% Calibrated, 6.3% Pseudo-precise), and present CheckMyCoach, a four-stage calibration pipeline.
